build.notes: Build completed successfully with expected asset size warnings (not blocking)

lint.notes: No Header-specific lint errors. All warnings are experimental API usage across the entire theme, not specific to this block

attributes.notes: Block hierarchy has correct attribute definitions: anchor, logoId, scroll behavior flags, search/SEP/mobile CTA links. All attributes properly typed in block.json

scenariosTested: Many nav items (8 items - overflow test)
edgeCases.notes: All 9 test scenarios present on test page with expected variations

interactiveBehavior.notes: Manual testing required for: mobile menu toggle, submenu open/close, keyboard navigation (ArrowDown/ArrowUp on buttons, Home/End in open submenus, Escape to close). JS breakpoint fix verified (changed from 48rem to 60rem to match theme md breakpoint).

checks.check: Submenu div has aria-label with parent title
Nav container has correct ID matching aria-controls
Form element for search with role='search'

keyboardNavigation.notes: JS implements: Tab navigation, Escape to close menus, ArrowDown/ArrowUp on closed submenu buttons (added in this revision), Home/End in open submenus (added in this revision), focus trapping in mobile menu

accessibility.notes: Keyboard navigation enhancements added: ArrowDown/ArrowUp support on submenu buttons when closed (opens and focuses first/last item), Home/End support within open submenus

markupDuplication.notes: No duplicated markup detected. Single UL for nav items with responsive classes (max-md:flex-col, md:flex-row). Single nav container with max-md: conditional collapse. Search form appears twice (desktop + mobile) but serves different contexts and only one is visible at a time.

checks.note: TSX and PHP both use: flex md:flex-row md:gap-8 md:items-start md:justify-end md:flex-wrap max-md:flex-col max-md:gap-8 max-md:w-full list-none p-0 m-0
TSX removed conditional wrapper to match PHP behavior
TSX: aria-label with link title + ' submenu'. PHP: aria-label with $linkTitle . ' submenu'
All conditional classes for fixed/hide-on-scroll match between TSX and PHP
PHP outputs data-fixed-scroll-desktop, data-fixed-scroll-mobile, data-hide-scroll-down-desktop, data-hide-scroll-down-mobile

structureSync.notes: TSX and PHP produce identical HTML structure: wrapper div, nav bar inner div, logo link, desktop right column, mobile toggle, collapsible nav container, UL for nav items

checks.check: Inner blocks content renders on frontend

specCompliance.notes: Test page properly structured with DemoContainer wrappers per current standards
